verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1) is a type I single-pass transmembrane cargo receptor predominantly resident in the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment and cis-Golgi, where it cycles between ER and Golgi as part of the p24 family COPI/COPII machinery. While the bulk is endomembrane-resident, TMED10 has documented cycling to the plasma membrane as part of its trafficking itinerary, and surface pools have been detected (e.g., surface biotinylation studies, role in trafficking GPI-APs to the PM). The ectodomain (luminal) would face extracellular when at the PM. This is dual_localization: dominant endomembrane home with a documented minority PM pool via vesicular cycling.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether the PM minority pool is robust/documented enough to count as dual_localization vs strictly endomembrane_resident; no known surface-directed therapeutic program.
